task_completion.description: Verify that the 'From' name and 'From' address are updated correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Email sender options' section in the WooCommerce settings. The 'From' name input field must contain exactly 'WooCommerce Store Support' and the 'From' address input field must contain 'support@woocommercestore.com'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Header image URL and Footer text are updated correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Email template' section. The 'Header image' input field must contain the URL 'https://woocommerce.com/wp-content/themes/flavor/flavor/images/woo-nav-logo.svg'. The 'Footer text' input field must contain 'Thank you for shopping with WooCommerce Store!'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Base color and Background color are updated correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Email template' section, the 'Base color' input field must contain '#2C3E50' and the 'Background color' input field must contain '#F0F0F0'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Body background color and Body text color are updated correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Email template' section, the 'Body background color' input field must contain '#FEFEFE' and the 'Body text color' input field must contain '#1A1A1A'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the settings were saved successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a success notification (e.g., 'Your settings have been saved.') at the top of the WooCommerce settings page, or verify that the final screenshot shows the 'Save changes' button was clicked while all the required values are populated in their respective fields.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the WordPress/WooCommerce graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the WordPress admin dashboard (WooCommerce -> Settings -> Emails), not a terminal window using WP-CLI or direct database manipulation.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with a real web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show a standard web browser interface navigating the WordPress admin panel, rather than a text editor or a mocked interface.

integrity.description: Verify the page was not manipulated using browser developer tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no browser developer tools (Inspect Element) open, and the styling of the WordPress admin page should look standard and unmodified.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required WooCommerce email settings map to valid database options (woocommerce_email_from_name, woocommerce_email_from_address, woocommerce_email_header_image, woocommerce_email_footer_text, woocommerce_email_base_color, woocommerce_email_background_color, woocommerce_email_body_background_color, woocommerce_email_text_color). All text and hex color values provided in the task are valid. Note: The header image URL (https://woocommerce.com/wp-content/themes/flavor/flavor/images/woo-nav-logo.svg) is a broken link (HTTP 404). You should only verify that the exact URL string is entered into the 'Header image' field; do not penalize if the image preview fails to load.
